Tests bitwise right shift assignment and equality operations on an object with custom methods.

# Purpose
This code defines an object with custom `valueOf` and `toString` methods, which are used to control how the object is converted to a primitive value. The `valueOf` method returns the number `16`, and the `toString` method returns the string representation `0`. The code then creates a reference `copyObject` to the same object. It uses a bitwise right shift assignment operation (`>>=`) on `object` and compares the result with a right shift operation (`>>`) on `copyObject`. An `assert` statement checks that the result of the shift assignment on `object` is equal to the result of the shift operation on `copyObject`, and that `object` itself is equal to the result of the shift operation on `copyObject`. This code tests the behavior of bitwise operations and object references in JavaScript.
